config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama; kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh dipakai untuk menginjak kebebasan dasar orang. Dalam pengalaman saya, alasan “demi masyarakat” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa, terutama kalau suara minoritas diabaikan. Yang lebih adil adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan kepentingan masyarakat di atas hak individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily become an excuse to silence people and ignore minority rights. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize social stability, public order, and collective responsibility, especially when a society wants to avoid chaos and keep development moving. But I also think individual rights need real protection, because without clear limits on power, "for the good of society" can be used too broadly and unfairly. The better principle is balance: society has legitimate interests, but those interests should not override basic rights unless there is a very strong and narrowly defined reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kritis, minoritas, atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa, padahal justru di situ hak individu paling perlu dilindungi. Stabilitas dan ketertiban memang penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar bisa disingkirkan seenaknya, itu bukan masyarakat yang sehat melainkan kekuasaan yang terlalu longgar. Menurut saya, yang benar adalah kepentingan publik dibatasi oleh hak-hak dasar, kecuali dalam keadaan yang benar-benar darurat dan sangat jelas alasannya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society's interests automatically higher than the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value stability, collective responsibility, and public order, because those things can matter a lot for development and daily life, but I also think that "for society" is too easy to use as a blanket justification for weak accountability. When individual rights are treated as secondary by default, minorities and ordinary people can lose protection very quickly, especially when power is not checked. So I would say society's interests matter, but they should be limited by basic rights, with exceptions only in truly necessary and clearly defined situations.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Di Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau melindungi pihak yang lebih berkuasa, jadi kalau hak individu dibuat nomor dua, penyalahgunaan sangat mudah terjadi. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tapi justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang melindungi hak dasar warganya, bukan yang mengorbankannya atas nama stabilitas. Pengecualian hanya layak dalam keadaan darurat yang benar-benar jelas, terbatas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as written, because society's interests should not automatically outrank individual rights. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the value placed on stability, public order, and collective goals, but I have also seen how easily "for the public good" can become a broad excuse for ignoring ordinary people's rights and weakening accountability. A healthy society is not one that asks individuals to sacrifice everything, but one that protects basic rights while pursuing common interests. In rare emergencies, some limits may be justified, but they must be narrow, transparent, and accountable, not a permanent principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
